title: Watch viral video: iShowSpeed cries inconsolably as Cristiano Ronaldo’s World Cup journey ends | Portugal vs Spain
content:
• Portugal was eliminated from the World Cup after a 1-0 defeat to Spain in a tense Round of 16 tie.
• Spain secured their 1-0 victory with the winning goal scored deep into stoppage time, specifically at the 90+1 minute.
• The defeat against Spain marked the end of Cristiano Ronaldo's World Cup career.

title: ‘Worst thing about Cristiano Ronaldo’: Messi fan’s farewell to CR7 goes viral after Portugal vs Spain | World Cup 2026
content:
• Lionel Messi subsequently won the FIFA World Cup 2022 with Argentina.
• Spain beat Portugal 1-0 at the World Cup, a defeat that effectively ended Cristiano Ronaldo's World Cup career.
• Cristiano Ronaldo has won 5 Ballon d'Or awards, fewer than Lionel Messi's 8.

title: INS Mahendragiri: All you need to know about Navy’s sixth Project 17A stealth frigate, to be commissioned on July 11
content:
• INS Mahendragiri (F38), the Indian Navy's sixth Project 17A indigenous stealth frigate, is set to be commissioned on July 11, 2026, at Visakhapatnam.
• INS Mahendragiri was designed by the Indian Navy’s Warship Design Bureau (WDB) and built by Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Limited (MDL) in Mumbai.
• With over 75 per cent indigenous content, INS Mahendragiri exemplifies the Government of India’s Aatmanirbhar Bharat initiative and was delivered to the Indian Navy on April 30, 2026.

title: Govt to sell up to 5.04% stake in Cochin Shipyard via OFS; floor price fixed at  ₹1,400 a share
content:
• The Centre will sell up to a 5.04% stake in Cochin Shipyard Ltd (CSL) via an Offer for Sale (OFS), with the floor price fixed at ₹1,400 per share.
• The government has set a disinvestment and asset monetisation target of ₹80,000 crore for the Union Budget FY27.
• As of now in FY27, the government has mobilised ₹24,928.09 crore towards its target, including ₹18,561.16 crore through disinvestment.

title: Trump’s ‘longtime friend’ wants White House to take control of midterm elections through emergency order: Report
content:
• Peter Ticktin, a lawyer and friend of Donald Trump, is urging the White House to declare a national emergency to place the 2026 midterm elections under federal control, citing unproven claims of foreign interference in the 2020 election.
• A 2021 US intelligence assessment found that while Russia, China, Iran, and Venezuela sought to influence public opinion surrounding the 2020 election, no foreign government attempted to alter voter registration systems or reported election results.
• Donald Trump's frustration is noted over Congress' failure to pass the SAVE America Act, which would impose stricter voter ID requirements.

title: Trump's immigration crackdown puts spouses of US citizens, marriage-based applicants under scrutiny: Report
content:
• The administration has directed financial institutions to review bank accounts belonging to people in the US without permanent legal status.
• Applicants for green cards are now expected to provide additional evidence, including proof of 'good moral character'.
• The Trump administration has introduced measures including pausing immigrant visas for people from 75 countries.

title: Adani assures continued control of Vizhinjam Port despite stake sale
content:
• Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone (APSEZ) has a binding agreement to sell a 49 per cent stake in Adani Vizhinjam Port Pvt Ltd (AVPPL) to Terminal Investment Ltd. (TIL), the port investment arm of MSC.
• The share transfer for the 49 per cent stake in AVPPL requires mandatory approvals from both the Kerala government and the Competition Commission of India (CCI).
• This partnership is described as bringing the single largest foreign private investment into India's port infrastructure and marks the third major collaboration between APSEZ and MSC after their joint ventures at Mundra's Container Terminal No. 3 and Ennore.

title: India Inc’s import dependence remains stable despite global supply risks: BoB report
content:
• A report by Bank of Baroda (BoB) stated that India Inc’s import dependence has remained broadly stable despite global supply risks.
• The BoB report assessed the import-to-net sales ratio of a sample of 1,372 companies, excluding banks and financial companies.
• The import intensity is concentrated in only a few sectors such as industrial gases and fuels, non-ferrous metals, and crude oil, rather than across the corporate sector.

title: Stealth frigate 'Mahendragiri' to be commissioned into Navy on July 11
content:
• Indigenous stealth frigate 'Mahendragiri', the sixth ship of the Nilgiri-class (Project 17A), is set to be commissioned into the Indian Navy on July 11.
• 'Mahendragiri' was designed by the Navy’s Warship Design Bureau (WDB) and built by Mazagon Dock Shipbuilders Limited (MDL) Mumbai.
• The 'Mahendragiri' frigate has over 75 per cent indigenous content, exemplifying the government of India’s Aatmanirbhar Bharat initiative.

title: Pinarayi Vijayan seeks SEBI probe into Vizhinjam Port stake sale
content:
• Adani Ports and Special Economic Zone (APSEZ) informed SEBI on June 29 about its definitive agreement to sell a 49% stake in its subsidiary, Adani Vizhinjam Port Pvt Ltd (AVPPL), to Terminal Investment Limited (TiL), the port-operating arm of the MSC Group.
• The concession agreement for Vizhinjam International Seaport stipulates that any transfer of 25% or more of the equity in the concessionaire requires prior approval from the Kerala government, a threshold exceeded by the proposed 49% stake sale.
• Kerala Leader of the Opposition Pinarayi Vijayan has urged SEBI to investigate the 49% stake sale in Adani Vizhinjam Port, directing the company to clarify its position under Regulation 30(11) of the Listing Obligations and Disclosure Requirements (LODR) Regulations.
